source.definition: to operate and control the movement of a vehicle or to travel in a vehicle to reach a destination

explanation.sourceText: The English verb “drive” originates from the Old English “drīfan,” meaning to push, urge, or force to move, especially animals or vehicles. Over time, it evolved to mean controlling the movement of a vehicle.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „drive” provine din engleza veche „drīfan”, care însemna a împinge, a îndemna sau a forța să se miște, în special animale sau vehicule. În timp, a evoluat să însemne controlarea mișcării unui vehicul.

source.additional: The word can have various meanings depending on context, such as operating a vehicle or moving in a particular direction.

target.definition: a se deplasa cu un vehicul sau a ajunge într-un loc

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb “a conduce” derives from the Latin “conducere,” meaning “to bring together, to lead,” and evolved to denote the action of driving or leading a vehicle.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a conduce“ derivă din latină, din „conducere“, care înseamnă „a aduce împreună, a conduce“, și a evoluat pentru a desemna acțiunea de a conduce un vehicul.

target.additional: Is frequently used when describing driving a vehicle or leading people.

examples.sourceText: I drive to work every day.
examples.targetText: Eu conduc în fiecare zi la muncă.

examples.sourceText: Are you driving to Berlin tomorrow?
We are driving to the sea this weekend.
examples.targetText: Noi conducem în weekend la mare.

examples.sourceText: They often drive to the mountains.
He used to drive to Germany often.
I would drive if I had time.
examples.targetText: Aș conduce dacă aș avea timp.

learningTips.text: Remember that “a conduce” means to lead or guide, which fits well with the English word “drive” since driving involves leading a vehicle.
Think of “conduce” as related to “conduct” or “conductor,” both meaning to lead or direct, helping you link it to driving.
Practice the conjugation aloud: “Eu conduc” (I drive), “El conduce” (he drives) to get comfortable with the forms.
Associate “conduce” with the English word “conduct” to remember it involves control and direction, just like driving.
